commits.message: Fix ASCII art line spacing in blog posts
commits.body:
The art had gaps between each row,
Like a picket fence letting wind blow.
line-height: 1 —
Now the lines touch, it's done,
Box-drawing chars in one seamless flow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
CSS border-left, you served us well,
But beside ASCII lines you looked like hell.
Now one <pre> holds the frame—
┌ │ └ — all the same,
No gaps, no seams, one monospace shell.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Proper Case nav, titles, and view-all links
commits.body:
The labels stood lowercase, meek,
Now Proper Case gives them mystique.
Two "view all" links appear,
Muted, quiet, but clear—
For the curious operator to seek.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add page titles to all routes
commits.body:
Every tab said "TermSurf" — nothing more,
No hint of the page you'd come for.
Now blog, docs, about,
Each title rings out,
Like nameplates on every front door.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add voice guidelines to website CLAUDE.md
commits.body:
The aesthetic had monospace and scan lines,
But the words had no rules, no guidelines.
Now we write like it's '93,
Dispatches from cyberspace, free—
No "leverage." No "seamless." Just punch lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add GitHub link to hero section
commits.body:
No download yet, no binary to ship,
But the source is there for the taking.
A bracket link whispers, "fork this,"
To every cypherpunk in the making.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Update hero copy with cypherpunk tone
commits.body:
Hack the web, not "control" it—
Unlimited power, keyboard-lit.
Chromium, WebKit, overlaid,
In your terminal, unafraid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add hero tagline to home page
commits.body:
Root access to the 'net—
A tagline the homepage now wears.
No more jumping to posts,
First we make our boasts,
Then the content beneath it declares.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add server entry for static file serving
commits.body:
The SSR handler knew how to render,
But static assets? "Not my agenda."
So server.ts now checks the disk
For CSS and JS — no more risk,
Then falls through to the Start contender.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Fix CSS not loading in production
commits.body:
The ?url import pointed nowhere,
Production assets lost in thin air.
A plain import instead—
The framework's well-read,
It injects the styles with great care.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The markdown wandered far from home,
In docs/blog it used to roam.
Now nestled where the website lives,
No copy hack the deploy forgives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The server was custom-built, bespoke,
A Bun SSR artisanal yoke.
TanStack Start took the wheel,
Now blog posts reveal
Their markdown from disk when invoked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add blog pages with markdown rendering
commits.body:
Remark parses, rehype highlights,
KaTeX waits for math-filled nights.
Blog layout, listing, post by slug—
Prose styled dark in the cypherpunk mug.
Feeds in the head, the reader delights.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A guide for agents yet to come,
On routes and themes and how things run.
Monospace, brackets, scanline haze—
All documented for future days.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add blog with first post and build pipeline
commits.body:
A post was born in docs/blog today,
TOML front matter showed it the way.
A script reads the markdown, feeds the machine,
Three feeds pour out — JSON, Atom, RSS stream.
The blog has a voice. Let it have its say.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Separate home page from blog page
commits.body:
Home is home and blog is blog,
Each deserves its own back-log.
Slash takes you where the landing lives,
Slash-blog is where the writing gives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add site navigation and page routes
commits.body:
Four brackets in a row they stand,
>[blog] [commits] [docs] [about] — the band.
The root now wraps, the welcome stays free,
Each page a route in the routing tree.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The dots were keen but out of line,
They clashed with text — a bad design.
The scanlines stay, the dots depart,
Sometimes less is better art.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Monospace glyphs and scanline haze,
Box-drawing borders, a brutalist phase.
Dot grids behind, no corners round,
[+] to expand what commits have found.
The terminal aesthetic, finally crowned.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Codesign warned of unsealed contents,
set -e took that as repentance.
The script died mid-stride,
webtui left outside—
Now || true grants it safe entrance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add light mode with Tokyo Night Day
commits.body:
The night was dark, the code was too,
But users in the sun had no clue.
Now Day is default, Night is dark—
prefers-color-scheme lights the spark.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Two issues, one overlay, zero bugs remain.
746 gave paint_pass the reins,
747 stopped the clobber chain.
Now every screen, every split, every pane—
The overlay lands where it should. No strain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Each frame swap clobbered the overlay's place,
A split-unaware formula set the wrong space.
Now it only positions on birth—
After that, paint_pass has worth,
And the second screen shows the right face.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Don't touch the function, move the call—
First creation needs it, swaps need none at all.
One branch to build, one branch to swap,
Only the first should position therop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Scale was zero, the branch fell through,
The else ran the same old code we knew.
Stored coords can't save you
When nothing yet gave you
A paint_pass with split-aware view.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Why recompute what you already know?
The pane recalls where overlays go.
If scale's been set before,
Don't recalculate once more—
Just trust the coordinates' flow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
We tried to delete the function,
Remove it with conviction—
But the overlay, bereft,
Sat at zero, zero, left,
Awaiting paint_pass benediction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Two functions fought to set one frame,
And neither knew the other's name.
paint_pass said "right!" — Chromium said "left!"
The user was of position bereft.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Screen one, screen two,
The overlay knew what to do—
Resize? No sweat.
Reposition? Not yet.
It needs a keypress as a cue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Five experiments walked into a bar.
Three stayed, two didn't get far.
The overlay now tracks the pane—
One screen's joy, two screens' pain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The split was async, the tree was new,
But the overlay sat, without a clue.
A notification sent — "Repaint, I say!"
One screen obliged, the other… went astray.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The split runs async, the mux stays mute,
No notification down the route.
One line to add: "WindowInvalidated!"
So paint_pass runs, the tree updated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The scale was one, it should be two,
DPI / DPI — wrong thing to do.
Divide by seventy-two instead,
And wrap in CATransaction (no animation dread).
The overlay lands right — unless you split,
Then async splits delay the fit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The scale said "one" — a blatant lie,
Default DPI matched the sky.
Both were one-forty-four,
Division gave us nothing more,
So the overlay went sailing by.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 746: add edge cases and checklist
commits.body:
Thirteen cases on the wall,
Thirteen cases to test—
One's crossed out (no screen at all),
But we'll verify the rest.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 746: use DPI for scale in experiment 3
commits.body:
The sublayer said "my scale is fine!"
But it never inherited the line.
Now we ask the DPI,
Same source, same reply—
One hundred forty-four over seventy-two: divine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
"Just return it," they said,
"The value's right there."
Two experiments wrote formulas instead—
Third time: read, don't declare.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A formula walked into a bar,
Said "I'll compute position by far!"
  paint_pane said "That's mine,"
  "You've crossed every line—
You're not even close to on par."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The first try lost the frame at birth,
The second guessed its scale on earth.
Now: place it once when layers appear,
Then paint_pass keeps it right from here—
With contentsScale, the truth, not math.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Two bugs, not three—the formula's fine,
Just search all tabs and reposition on switch time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
We moved the overlay to paint_pass,
But the CALayer showed up—alas—
At zero, zero, bare,
Then too far down from there.
You can't defer what needs a first class.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Seventy-two was fine for Mac alone,
But Linux runs at ninety-six, we've known.
Pass DPI in, let the function divide—
default_dpi() picks the right side.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Backing pixels in, points come out,
DPI over seventy-two, no doubt.
The scale was a mystery, now it's clear—
Same source the whole renderer holds dear.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Two formulas fought for one pane's fate,
The render pass knew, but arrived too late.
Now paint_pass() shall set the frame—
One truth, one path, one positioning game.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The overlay drifts when tabs are switched,
Its formula from paint_pane() ditched.
Three bugs conspire to move the frame—
One source of truth shall end the game.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The commits grew stale, the dates were wrong,
The hashes no longer sang their song.
A fresh JSON pour, the feed renewed—
Now termsurf.com shows the latest brood.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
We searched the seas of Git and found,
No database keeps repos sound.
Bare files on NVMe, tried and true—
A SpinServer in Dallas will do!

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Debian or Ubuntu, which to choose?
With apt on both, you cannot lose.
But Ubuntu's docs are vast and clear,
Twenty-four-oh-four LTS is here—
Five years of support, no excuse.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Five hosts in Dallas, side by side,
NVMe and RAM, the specs compared wide.
Spin gives the most bang for the buck,
OVH plays it safe—pick your luck.
Two finalists stand, the rest step aside.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The database dream was tempting, true,
But filesystems do what databases can't do.
OVH in Dallas, NVMe and bare,
Unmetered bandwidth beyond compare—
One box to host them all, brand new.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles
